verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF-family secreted peptide hormone that acts as a ligand for EGFR and ERBB4. The NCBI summary and standard biomedical literature establish it as a soluble secreted factor. There is no evidence of a membrane-anchored isoform, GPI anchor, or stable post-translational surface tethering. EREG binds its receptors on the cell surface via reversible non-covalent interaction with secreted-form receptor ligands — a classical soluble cytokine interaction. The protein body itself does not have a stable physical link to the plasma membrane.
